sn44.3:1.1: On one occasion Ven. Sāriputta and Ven. Mahā Koṭṭhita were staying near Vārāṇasī in the Deer Park at Isipatana.
sn44.3:1.2: Then Ven. Mahā Koṭṭhita, emerging from his seclusion in the evening, went to Ven. Sāriputta and exchanged courteous greetings with him.
sn44.3:1.3: After an exchange of friendly greetings & courtesies, he sat to one side. As he was sitting there, he said to Ven. Sāriputta,
sn44.3:2.1: “Now then, friend Sāriputta, does the Tathāgata exist after death?”
sn44.3:2.2: “That, friend, has not been declared by the Blessed One:
sn44.3:2.4: “Well then, friend Sāriputta, does the Tathāgata not exist after death?”
sn44.3:2.5: “Friend, that too has not been declared by the Blessed One:
sn44.3:2.6: ‘The Tathāgata does not exist after death.’”
sn44.3:2.7: “Then does the Tathāgata both exist and not exist after death?”
sn44.3:2.8: “That has not been declared by the Blessed One:
sn44.3:2.9: ‘The Tathāgata both exists and does not exist after death.’”
sn44.3:2.10: “Well then, does the Tathāgata neither exist nor not exist after death?”
sn44.3:2.11: “That too has not been declared by the Blessed One:
sn44.3:2.12: ‘The Tathāgata neither exists nor does not exist after death.’”
sn44.3:3.1: “Now, friend Sāriputta, when asked if the Tathāgata exists after death, you say, ‘That has not been declared by the Blessed One:
sn44.3:3.2: “The Tathāgata exists after death.”’ When asked if the Tathāgata does not exist after death… both exists and does not exist after death…
sn44.3:3.3: neither exists nor does not exist after death, you say,
sn44.3:3.4: ‘That too has not been declared by the Blessed One:
sn44.3:3.5: “The Tathāgata neither exists nor does not exist after death.”’
sn44.3:3.6: Now, what is the cause, what is the reason, why that has not been declared by the Blessed One?”
sn44.3:4.1: “‘The Tathāgata exists after death’ is immersed in form.
sn44.3:4.2: ‘The Tathāgata does not exist after death’ is immersed in form.
sn44.3:4.3: ‘The Tathāgata both exists and does not exist after death’ is immersed in form.
sn44.3:4.4: ‘The Tathāgata neither exists nor does not exist after death’ is immersed in form.
sn44.3:4.5: “‘The Tathāgata exists after death’ is immersed in feeling…
sn44.3:4.9: “‘The Tathāgata exists after death’ is immersed in perception…
sn44.3:4.13: “‘The Tathāgata exists after death’ is immersed in fabrication…
sn44.3:4.17: “‘The Tathāgata exists after death’ is immersed in consciousness.
sn44.3:4.18: ‘The Tathāgata does not exist after death’ is immersed in consciousness.
sn44.3:4.19: ‘The Tathāgata both exists and does not exist after death’ is immersed in consciousness.
sn44.3:4.20: ‘The Tathāgata neither exists nor does not exist after death’ is immersed in consciousness.
sn44.3:4.21: “This is the cause, this is the reason, why that has not been declared by the Blessed One.”
